content.full_text:
AMERICAN SAVINGS BANK

MARGERY HINTERMAN
Paralegal

Direct Dial (714) 252-4773
Fax (714) 252-4799

March 15, 1991

Ms. Lynda Cassady
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Merger of Affiliated PACs
American Savings Bank PAC FPPC #891056
American Savings Bank Employee PAC FPPC #891082

Dear Ms. Cassady:

I spoke with you several months ago about merging the two referenced Political Action Committees. In order to deplete the balance of funds of PAC FPPC #891082, contributions have been made to candidates, etc. and no member contributions have been received since May 1990. PAC FPPC #891056 is the Association's PAC that has been receiving contributions from its members. Because dual reporting is duplicative and time-consuming, it would be to our advantage as well as yours to merge the two PACs.

According to our telephone conversation, I understand we may transfer the entire balance of one PAC to the other by filing a Form 415 with a Form 420. The "transfer out" of the balance of PAC #891082 will be shown on Schedule E and the "transfer in" to Pac #891056 will be shown on Schedule G. Form 420 will show a "0" balance.

If you confirm that the procedure described above is correct, please sign the enclosed copy of this letter and return it to me in the enclosed self-addressed envelope. If incorrect, please call me at (714) 252-4773 and discuss the correct procedure with me.

I appreciate you assistance in this matter.

Sincerely,

Margery Hinterman
Paralegal

17877 Von Karman, P.O. Box 19689, Irvine, California 92713-9689 (714) 252-4709

Lynda Cassady
Fair Political Practices Commission
March 15, 1991
page 2


CONFIRMED


_________________________________          _________________________________
Name                                       Date

Enclosure

MH

cc:    George R. Bravante, Jr., Treasurer (w/o enc.)
       William D. Hoel, Chairman (w/o enc.)

sections.question_synthetic: What is the correct procedure for merging two affiliated Political Action Committees (PACs) and transferring the fund balance from one PAC to another?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request from a bank seeking FPPC guidance, not an FPPC advice letter. The document shows 'CONFIRMED' signature line on page 2, suggesting it may have been signed by Ms. Cassady, but contains no formal FPPC response text. No standard question/conclusion sections present. Treated as correspondence rather than formal advice letter due to lack of FPPC-authored response content.

embedding.summary: American Savings Bank's paralegal seeks confirmation from the FPPC regarding the proper procedure for merging two affiliated PACs (FPPC #891056 and #891082) by transferring the entire balance using Forms 415 and 420 with appropriate schedule entries.
